hazard.summary: CLI surfaces 429 as a generic “API Error” without distinguishing throughput vs usage/quota limits or providing actionable retry/cap guidance, causing avoidable thrash and slow diagnosis.
signals: User-reported: rate limit errors show "API Error"
Need: concurrency cap guidance + better logging/traceability

constraints: 429 handling MUST distinguish limit type when possible (throughput vs quota) and surface the classification to the user.
If Retry-After is present, client MUST respect it and avoid re-hitting the same provider/model until cooldown expires.
Retries MUST be bounded by max elapsed / attempt caps; avoid infinite or fast-loop retry behavior.
Client SHOULD provide an actionable hint: reduce concurrency, enable backoff+jitter, and include a stable request/attempt identifier in logs.
verification: Repro: induce 429s (burst requests) and confirm error output labels the limit type (throughput vs quota) when possible.
If Retry-After is returned, confirm the client sleeps/cools down and does not immediately re-hit the same endpoint.
Confirm concurrency cap guidance is present (and ideally configurable) and reduces 429 recurrence under load.
Confirm retries obey max_elapsed / max_attempts and produce a clear terminal error when exhausted.
Confirm logs include request/attempt identifiers sufficient to correlate user reports with rate-limit events.
